vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1319

plan.title: bug(scripts/reconcile_issues): --apply-lifecycle-fixes over-classifies decomposition children whose planRef is OPEN but decomposition_origin parent is CLOSED (v0.33.0 cut misclassified #1283 #1284 #1285 #1291)

narratives.Description: task reconcile:issues --apply-lifecycle-fixes over-eagerly moves decomposition-child vBRIEFs to completed/ when their decomposition_origin parent is closed, even though each child's own primary planRef issue is still OPEN. The v0.33.0 cut misclassified #1283, #1284, #1285, and #1291 this way; this story tightens the move gate to key only on the primary planRef so the gate catches real drift instead of producing false drift.
narratives.Overview:
## Problem

`task reconcile:issues -- --apply-lifecycle-fixes` (script: `scripts/reconcile_issues.py`) makes over-eager `proposed/` / `pending/` / `cancelled/` -> `completed/` moves for vBRIEFs whose **primary `planRef` issue is OPEN** but whose `metadata.x-tracking.decomposition_origin` parent is CLOSED. The heuristic appears to treat a closed-parent signal as a closure signal for the children, ignoring that decomposition children carry their own lifecycle.

## Recurrence record

v0.33.0 release cut (master `66420c2` -> `0fc35e8` -> `0fc35e8`), 2026-05-22. The Phase 1.6 reconcile gate (per `skills/deft-directive-release/SKILL.md`) misclassified 4 vBRIEFs as completed despite their primary `planRef` issues being OPEN with active work:

- `#1283` -- pack-slicing RFC; GH OPEN; labels `design`, `rfc`; body "Status: Open for design discussion"; `kind: research`; items pending; ADR pointer doesn t exist.
- `#1284` -- Epic 1 umbrella (vBRIEF-as-canonical); GH OPEN; labels `documentation`, `epic`, `determinism`, `status:tracker`; 11 children across 4 waves actively being worked; items pending.
- `#1285` -- Epic 2 umbrella (CRUD harness); GH OPEN; labels `epic`, `determinism`, `status:tracker`; 4 children across 2 waves actively being worked; items pending.
- `#1291` -- ADR-001 doc deliverable; GH OPEN; labels `documentation`, `rfc`, `determinism`; `kind: story`; `swarm.readiness: not-ready`; ADR file unwritten; all 3 items pending.

All 4 share `metadata.x-tracking.decomposition_origin = #742`, which IS closed. The reconciler appears to use that closed-origin signal as the move trigger.

## Reproduction

```bash
# Pre-state: 4 vBRIEFs in proposed/ + pending/, all with planRef issues OPEN on GitHub
task reconcile:issues -- --apply-lifecycle-fixes
# Output: "vBRIEFs reconciled (moved=5, already-completed=N, failures=0)"
# Surfaces "(Issue is closed)" for the closed PARENT #742 against each of the 4 child vBRIEF paths
# git status shows 4 renames into vbrief/completed/ + 1 from cancelled/ for #742
```

Observed during the v0.33.0 release cut at 2026-05-22T19:13Z. Recovery (this issue s parent commit) reverted the 4 moves manually.

## Root cause hypothesis

The lifecycle-fix heuristic likely walks `references[]` (or some equivalent set) and treats ANY closed-issue reference as a completion signal. For decomposition children, the `decomposition_origin` parent is in `references[]` (cross-link) and is closed because it was decomposed away. The heuristic should consult ONLY the vBRIEF s primary `planRef` (or equivalent canonical-parent-issue) field, not the broader reference set.

## Proposed fix

- Tighten the move-to-completed gate so it only fires when the vBRIEF s **primary `planRef`** issue is closed on GitHub AND the vBRIEF lives in a non-completed lifecycle folder.
- Cross-references in `references[]` and `metadata.x-tracking.decomposition_origin` MUST NOT trigger the move on their own.
- Add a unit test covering the decomposition-child case (parent closed, child planRef open) to lock the gate s behaviour.
- Consider surfacing a `--dry-run` preview before mutation -- the apply-mode flag is destructive at scale and the recurrence record shows the heuristic can misfire on 4 vBRIEFs in a single run.
- Cross-reference the v0.33.0 release skill Phase 1.6 (`skills/deft-directive-release/SKILL.md` Phase 1 Step 6) -- it documents this gate as the operator s first-line defence against lifecycle drift; if the heuristic can over-classify, the gate is producing FALSE drift rather than catching real drift.

## Cross-references

- v0.33.0 release commit `0fc35e8`
- Reconcile recovery commit (this issue s parent commit; will reference once written)
- #742 (closed parent that triggers the false positive)
- #1283, #1284, #1285, #1291 (the 4 false-positive children)
- #1290 (`feat(vbrief): reconcile_issues honors stateReason` -- adjacent feature work on the same script; possible coordination opportunity)
- `scripts/reconcile_issues.py` (the implementation)
- `skills/deft-directive-release/SKILL.md` Phase 1 Step 6 (the consumer surface)
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
- In scripts/reconcile_issues.py, tighten the move-to-completed gate so it fires only when the vBRIEF's primary planRef issue is closed on GitHub; a closed references[] cross-link or a closed metadata.x-tracking.decomposition_origin parent MUST NOT trigger the move on its own.
- Consult only the canonical primary-parent-issue field when deciding the lifecycle move, not the broader reference set, so decomposition children with an OPEN planRef stay put even when their decomposition_origin parent is CLOSED.
- Add a regression test in tests/cli/test_reconcile_issues.py covering the decomposition-child case (parent closed, child planRef open) asserting no move occurs; verify it fails before the fix.
- Add a CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] / ### Fixed entry in #1242 style referencing #1319.
narratives.UserStory: As a maintainer running task reconcile:issues --apply-lifecycle-fixes during a release cut, I want decomposition children whose own planRef issue is still OPEN to stay in their lifecycle folder even when the decomposition parent is closed, so that the gate catches real drift instead of producing false drift.
narratives.Approach: 2026-06-17 file-overlap audit: touches scripts/reconcile_issues.py + tests/cli/test_reconcile_issues.py only. The #924 item-status fix already merged to master on this same file; this carrier rebases on that. Disjoint from the other cohort carriers (doctor, triage-filter, skills). Parallel-safe; no depends_on. Recurrence ground-truth: v0.33.0 cut misclassified #1283/#1284/#1285/#1291 (all decomposition_origin=#742, closed).

items.title: Move-to-completed gate fires only on a closed primary planRef issue.

narrative.Acceptance: Given a vBRIEF whose primary planRef issue is OPEN, when apply_lifecycle_fixes runs, then it is NOT moved to completed/ regardless of closed cross-links (asserted in tests/cli/test_reconcile_issues.py).

items.title: Closed decomposition_origin parent does not trigger a child move.

narrative.Acceptance: Given a child vBRIEF with OPEN planRef and a CLOSED metadata.x-tracking.decomposition_origin parent, when apply_lifecycle_fixes runs, then no move occurs (regression test asserts this).

items.title: Regression test for the decomposition-child case.

narrative.Acceptance: Given the test suite, when pytest tests/cli/test_reconcile_issues.py runs, then it covers parent-closed/child-planRef-open and fails before the fix.
Given the diff, when reviewed, then CHANGELOG.md has an [Unreleased] / ### Fixed entry referencing #1319.

references.title: Issue #1319: bug(scripts/reconcile_issues): --apply-lifecycle-fixes over-classifies decomposition children whose planRef is OPEN but decomposition_origin parent is CLOSED (v0.33.0 cut misclassified #1283 #1284 #1285 #1291)

expected_outputs: move-to-completed gate keys only on primary planRef closure
decomposition-child (parent closed, child open) is not moved
swarm.missing_traces_justification: Bug ingested from GitHub issue #1319; no spec-section trace applies. File scope disjoint from other cohort carriers.
